---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 5a4ea5b02b3590cebe11d7ef04f683c1c1897c7e
findings:
  - { id: judged-j-1, kind: judged, text: "ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a full frontmatter re-render (align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter over a value-copied struct), not a byte splice: on a strict-decode-legal living report whose frontmatter formatting differs from the canonical renderer (block-style findings list, alternate quoting), every OTHER finding's line and the digest/integrity/judge_integrity lines are silently rewritten into canonical form. The spec's two operational verifier checks (ComputeDigest equality, VerifyIntegrity success) still hold since they are value-level, and align-generated reports are always canonical, so this only diverges on hand-authored-but-legal reports — the letter of 'byte-for-byte' is weaker than the implementation guarantees in that corner. (confidence 0.30)" }
  - { id: judged-j-2, kind: judged, text: "The body update locates the target finding's old rendered line by raw substring count (strings.Count(bodyStr, oldLine) in cmd/verdi/disposition.go:189) rather than whole-line matching. If a previously written rationale quotes another finding's full rendered bullet verbatim (the note is embedded raw into the body line by RenderFindingLine), the quoted finding's old line then occurs twice in the body and every later attempt to disposition it exits 2 as an 'internal error' — a permanent, non-amendable dead end for that finding with no sanctioned escape hatch, reachable purely through the verb's own documented inputs. Refusal is honest (no partial write), but ac-1's promise that the verb can record a disposition on any finding in a living report is not met for that state. (confidence 0.30)" }
  - { id: judged-j-3, kind: judged, text: "--rationale text is embedded unescaped into the single-line body bullet (RenderFindingLine appends ' — \u003cnote\u003e' raw) while the frontmatter note is YAMLDoubleQuote-escaped; a rationale containing a newline produces a multi-line 'bullet', so the human-legible body no longer renders as the one line the machine record corresponds to, and a crafted second line starting with '- **' can visually impersonate another finding. Argument parsing performs no newline/shape validation, so this passes silently with exit 0. (confidence 0.25)" }
digest: sha256:66752ae7a8990ee6eda0ba15658428da791b30f5458f1f91206c8a3d34961c40
integrity: sha256:75f4a37ac39ea75f6de30ed5100598d0d1037ef12b6a9489a6f981bb4d4407c7
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"j-1\",\"text\":\"ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a full frontmatter re-render (align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter over a value-copied struct), not a byte splice: on a strict-decode-legal living report whose frontmatter formatting differs from the canonical renderer (block-style findings list, alternate quoting), every OTHER finding's line and the digest/integrity/judge_integrity lines are silently rewritten into canonical form. The spec's two operational verifier checks (ComputeDigest equality, VerifyIntegrity success) still hold since they are value-level, and align-generated reports are always canonical, so this only diverges on hand-authored-but-legal reports — the letter of 'byte-for-byte' is weaker than the implementation guarantees in that corner.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"j-2\",\"text\":\"The body update locates the target finding's old rendered line by raw substring count (strings.Count(bodyStr, oldLine) in cmd/verdi/disposition.go:189) rather than whole-line matching. If a previously written rationale quotes another finding's full rendered bullet verbatim (the note is embedded raw into the body line by RenderFindingLine), the quoted finding's old line then occurs twice in the body and every later attempt to disposition it exits 2 as an 'internal error' — a permanent, non-amendable dead end for that finding with no sanctioned escape hatch, reachable purely through the verb's own documented inputs. Refusal is honest (no partial write), but ac-1's promise that the verb can record a disposition on any finding in a living report is not met for that state.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"j-3\",\"text\":\"--rationale text is embedded unescaped into the single-line body bullet (RenderFindingLine appends ' — \u003cnote\u003e' raw) while the frontmatter note is YAMLDoubleQuote-escaped; a rationale containing a newline produces a multi-line 'bullet', so the human-legible body no longer renders as the one line the machine record corresponds to, and a crafted second line starting with '- **' can visually impersonate another finding. Argument parsing performs no newline/shape validation, so this passes silently with exit 0.\",\"confidence\":0.25}]}" }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-j-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a full frontmatter re-render (align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter over a value-copied struct), not a byte splice: on a strict-decode-legal living report whose frontmatter formatting differs from the canonical renderer (block-style findings list, alternate quoting), every OTHER finding's line and the digest/integrity/judge_integrity lines are silently rewritten into canonical form. The spec's two operational verifier checks (ComputeDigest equality, VerifyIntegrity success) still hold since they are value-level, and align-generated reports are always canonical, so this only diverges on hand-authored-but-legal reports — the letter of 'byte-for-byte' is weaker than the implementation guarantees in that corner. (confidence 0.30)
- **judged-j-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: The body update locates the target finding's old rendered line by raw substring count (strings.Count(bodyStr, oldLine) in cmd/verdi/disposition.go:189) rather than whole-line matching. If a previously written rationale quotes another finding's full rendered bullet verbatim (the note is embedded raw into the body line by RenderFindingLine), the quoted finding's old line then occurs twice in the body and every later attempt to disposition it exits 2 as an 'internal error' — a permanent, non-amendable dead end for that finding with no sanctioned escape hatch, reachable purely through the verb's own documented inputs. Refusal is honest (no partial write), but ac-1's promise that the verb can record a disposition on any finding in a living report is not met for that state. (confidence 0.30)
- **judged-j-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: --rationale text is embedded unescaped into the single-line body bullet (RenderFindingLine appends ' — <note>' raw) while the frontmatter note is YAMLDoubleQuote-escaped; a rationale containing a newline produces a multi-line 'bullet', so the human-legible body no longer renders as the one line the machine record corresponds to, and a crafted second line starting with '- **' can visually impersonate another finding. Argument parsing performs no newline/shape validation, so this passes silently with exit 0. (confidence 0.25)
